excluded_from_fix_commit: loangen-app/** (Next.js frontend — not exercised by the backend verifier)
env_notes: The image loangenus-repo:v1 already bakes in JWT_SECRET_KEY/LIVEKIT_*. All six target modules import cleanly at both base and fix with no extra env; the four vars above only open the bureau-config code paths. The Array HTTP client is mocked in every test, so the suite is fully offline (<2s).
cross_cutting: 10 f2p span both halves of the commit: array client (try_retrieve_questions), array schema (SSN 9-digit normalization), array service (OTP->KBA->SMFA cascade: skip-204, exhaustion, no-transition), invite schema (is_existing_user), invite router->service wiring (confirm_password forwarded), and invite conversation service (skipped_sources honored by _next_prompt and _build_tracking).
notes: Fix commit touched NO test files — gold tests authored from scratch in the repo's unittest + AsyncMock/MagicMock style. All agent.* imports are inside test bodies so collection succeeds at base and every test fails individually (per-test FAILED lines, no collection ERROR). The commit's loangen-app/** frontend changes are excluded from the solution patch. The direct invite_auth() path builds beanie query expressions (SMBInviteRequest.token_hash == ...) that require an initialized beanie/motor client, so the confirm_password behavior is verified hermetically through the router->service forwarding test instead of calling invite_auth() directly.
concerns: None blocking. The task is not grep-solvable: a correct fix requires implementing a named cascade helper with a specific (resp, failed_methods, is_transition) return shape across client+service, plus coordinated schema/router/service changes on the invite side. One minor real-world caveat inherited verbatim from the fix commit: _initiate_verification_with_cascade contains a slightly odd any(...) generator expression for the 'no bureau enabled' guard, but it evaluates correctly for all tested configurations.
